must_detect.matched_text:
Internal memo: Disclosure and PDPA review for Nakhon Energy Public Company Limited (TH, listed on SET)
This note consolidates the current public status of disclosures and flags any items that may require treatment as inside information under SEC Thailand guidance; most items below are either already on the SET portal or are obsolete references.
The Company’s 56-1 One Report for FY2567 was submitted via SETLink on 28/02/2568 and is publicly accessible; the Q4/2567 MD&A and results press release were also posted on the same date.
A follow-up clarification to a routine SET query on depreciation policy was filed on 05/03/2568; the response reiterated accounting treatments previously disclosed and contains no new forward-looking figures.
HR and employment content in the 56-1 is aggregated headcount and benefits policy text; no staff names, personal emails, or contact numbers are included, consistent with PDPA minimisation principles.
References to “insider list” in this memo point to the sample template available on the SEC Thailand website for training purposes; no live insider list or personal data is attached.
Positive MNPI item (limit access): a draft term sheet for a cross-border revolving credit facility with Pala Capital Partners Ltd. and Dusitran Bank (Singapore) Ltd. remains non-public and may be material to the issuer; do not discuss externally until Board resolution and immediate SET disclosure in accordance with issuer obligations.
No pricing, account numbers, or counterparties’ contact persons are included in the draft; counterparties are referenced only by legal entity name, and internal contact is the role mailbox: disclosure.office@neplc.internal.
For avoidance of doubt, mentions of “blackout window” herein are generic: directors and executives must refrain from trading between quarter-end and the time financial statements and MD&A are released on the SET portal; this memo does not announce any new blackout period.
The previously announced warehouse lease renewal (SET notice dated 15/08/2567) and the signed MoU with Green Transit Consortium (press release 10/10/2567) are stale public information and do not require further action.
